title: Decalobanthus boisianus
intro: Decalobanthus boisianus, also known as Boisian's decalobanthus, is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of D. boisianus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with D. boisianus include decoctions, infusions, and standardized extracts, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Decalobanthus boisianus is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes over 600 genera and 13,000 species. Decalobanthus boisianus is a species of flowering plant, and its specific classification may be further detailed within the subfamily Rubioideae. Further research would be needed to determine the exact classification of Decalobanthus boisianus at the genus and species level. Its classification is part of a broader understanding of plant taxonomy and evolution within the Rubiaceae family.
